strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience across multiple senior roles, with React and AWS CDK demonstrated at Optilyz and current role at re:cinq
Active AI-agent and MCP (Model Context Protocol) infrastructure development at current role directly satisfies the explicit AI agent development requirement
Cloud and containerization experience is well-evidenced: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes-deployable platform at GISCollective, Docker at Altom
concerns: Nest.js is the listed primary backend framework but is entirely absent from the resume — no evidence of familiarity with its module/DI architecture

Testing framework specifics (Vitest, Jest, Playwright) are never named; TDD practice is implied at RIDE Capital and via the Fluent-Asserts/Trial D-language projects, but no JS/TS test tooling is cited
Kotlin/Java exposure is minimal (robotic arms at Altom, 2016–2018) and secondary stack requirement is effectively unmet; relational database depth (PostgreSQL/MySQL) is also not demonstrated
response.justification: Bogdan brings over 15 years of fullstack experience with a strong TypeScript/Node.js/React core that maps directly to the primary stack, and his current role at re:cinq is the most compelling signal: it explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, which is a rare and directly required competency. AWS CDK and Kubernetes experience satisfy the cloud/containerization bar. The main gaps are Nest.js (unlisted entirely, despite being the primary backend framework), named JS/TS testing tooling, and meaningful Kotlin/Java work — the Kotlin secondary requirement is essentially unmet. These are learnable gaps for a developer of his caliber, so they reduce confidence without disqualifying. Score derived from: two high-weight positives (AI agents, TS/Node/React depth) offset by one medium-weight negative (Nest.js) and two lower-weight gaps (testing tooling names, Kotlin).
